codeReview.summary: The feature adds the three required debug scripts under research/debug/ and they align with the mission goal of network capture, pcap analysis, and strace-based tracing. Implementation quality is generally solid and self-documenting, with two non-blocking reliability issues noted below.

issues.description: In the nsenter fallback path, the script exits with the raw strace/timeout code. A normal duration expiry returns 124, so successful captures can be reported as failures to callers.
When --output is provided, the script writes directly to Path(args.output) without creating parent directories, which raises FileNotFoundError for nested output paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The debug-worker skill's test step is not aligned with this mission's known pre-existing failures, which led the worker to run an unfiltered pytest command and hit a known failure before any edits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill says to run `uv run pytest tests/runner/ -x -q` (.factory/skills/debug-worker/SKILL.md:26). Mission AGENTS documents pre-existing failures and filtered invocation guidance (/home/ubuntu/.factory/missions/ba94244c-8343-4186-8ee5-9d59460ef8a8/AGENTS.md:76-80). Transcript skeleton shows the worker ran unfiltered runner tests, then noted a pre-existing failure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared guidance is internally inconsistent on test filtering for known failures.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS lists two known failing tests but its suggested command excludes only `test_provenance_chain_documented` (/home/ubuntu/.factory/missions/ba94244c-8343-4186-8ee5-9d59460ef8a8/AGENTS.md:76-80), while .factory/services.yaml test command excludes both known failures.

summary: Reviewed feature debug-scripts (worker session ae0db73a-4285-47a0-a00e-caf2f3beb10b, commit a55138e). The feature satisfies its required scope and is marked pass, with two non-blocking script robustness issues and two shared-state documentation/skill alignment observations.
